title: worker-log-digest output data
description:
The code-rendered digest of one worker log.

On an unreadable/missing file the verb FAILS OPEN: ``readable`` is False,
``error`` names the problem, the counts/tail are empty, and ``render``
states it plainly — never a traceback.

log_path.description: The resolved absolute path that was digested.
log_path.title: Log Path

exists.description: Whether the log file exists on disk.
exists.title: Exists

readable.description: Whether the log file could be read.
readable.title: Readable

error.description: Fail-open diagnostic when the file is missing/unreadable; else null.
error.title: Error

total_lines.description: Total line count of the log (0 when unreadable).
total_lines.title: Total Lines

tail_lines_requested.description: The tail_lines the caller asked for (echoed back for provenance).
tail_lines_requested.title: Tail Lines Requested

marker_counts.description: Per-marker count of lines CONTAINING that known engine marker, keyed by the exact marker string. Empty when unreadable. Every known marker is present (0 when absent) so the shape is stable.
marker_counts.title: Marker Counts

tail.description: The last tail_lines lines of the log, VERBATIM (newlines stripped).
tail.title: Tail

render.description: Deterministic markdown digest (counts + verbatim fenced tail) the caller relays VERBATIM — never re-interpreted into freeform prose.
render.title: Render
